principle: Match every page to one of three query intents — navigational (find a brand), informational (learn something), or transactional (buy/act) — a mismatched intent between keyword and page is why high-traffic pages generate zero conversions
explanation: X.seo|query_intent: three_types: navigational(find_specific_site/brand)+informational(learn+research+non-commercial)+transactional(buy+sign_up+download); traffic_value: transactional=very_high+informational=medium+navigational=medium; every_landing_page_should_target_one_intent; keyword_trap: iphone_14_data_plans_on_phone_case_site -> traffic_with_zero_conversion; NO:targeting_transactional_keywords_with_informational_content NO:one_page_trying_to_serve_all_three_intents; eg: diabetes=informational >> buy_insulin_online=transactional -> require_separate_pages

principle: Score each keyword on conversion alignment, not just search volume — a 200-search keyword from buyers ready to act is worth more than a 50,000-search keyword that attracts non-buyers
explanation: X.seo|keyword_relevance: relevance_scoring: 1-3_scale_based_on_intent_alignment+conversion_goal_match+content_match; high_volume_not_equal_high_value; keyword_phases: discovery(informational)+evaluation(comparison)+purchase(transactional); build_separate_optimized_pages_for_each_phase; avoid_third-party_relevance_scores -> develop_own_based_on_customer_avatars+goals; NO:targeting_keyword_by_search_volume_alone NO:one_page_targeting_all_intent_phases; eg: best_camera_phones=informational >> buy_iPhone_15_Pro_unlocked=transactional -> different_pages_needed

principle: 95% of all keywords are long-tail — they represent 35% of total search volume and higher conversion rates, making content depth the primary driver of organic traffic share
explanation: X.seo|long_tail: data: 95%_of_all_keywords=long_tail(Ahrefs); represent=35%_of_total_search_volume; characteristics: 4-7_words+specific_questions+higher_conversion_rates; evidence: WebMD_beats_Mayo_Clinic_in_traffic+NerdWallet_beats_Fidelity -> both_win_via_depth+breadth_of_content; Bloomreach: 75%_of_users_time_on_sites=discovery_mode; NO:targeting_only_high-volume_head_terms NO:thin_content_pages_for_single_keyword; eg: diabetes >> what_can_diabetics_eat_for_breakfast -> long_tail_higher_purchase_intent

principle: Sites with greater breadth and depth of content around a topic win the most traffic — a site with 24,000 topic-specific pages will beat a competitor with 1,400 pages on the same subject
explanation: X.seo|content_breadth_depth: principle: more_topic_pages=more_long_tail_coverage=more_traffic; evidence: NerdWallet_24000_pages_vs_Fidelity_1460_pages -> NerdWallet_wins_traffic+trust; Google_messy_middle: users_rarely_go_straight_from_query_to_purchase -> many_related_questions_must_be_answered_along_the_way; if_site_doesnt_answer_sub-questions -> user_leaves_and_converts_elsewhere; NO:launching_site_with_10_pages_and_buying_ads NO:creating_content_only_for_main_service_keywords; eg: insurance_company_blog_10_posts >> 500_question-answering_posts -> dominates_search

principle: Think of your site's information architecture as a filing cabinet — organize content from broad categories down to specific detail pages so search engines can learn the semantic relationships between your topics
explanation: X.seo|ia_filing_cabinet: hierarchy: home(broadest) -> category -> subcategory -> detail_page(most_specific); search_engines: crawl_semantic_relationships_between_pages -> broad-to-specific_hierarchy_reinforces_topical_relevance; protocol: list_all_pages_first -> create_top_nav_that_holds_all_content_types -> reverse_design_top-down; logical_category_flow: category_pages_link_to_sub_pages+detail_pages_link_back; NO:organically_grown_site_with_no_IA_plan NO:mixing_unrelated_topics_under_same_category; eg: /dogs/breeds/golden-retriever -> Google_understands_hierarchy_and_topic
